review_summary: Inline lambda per item. Fine for normal cases; if Button is memo'd, defeats.

issues_found.title: OK for non-memo'd
issues_found.explanation: For simple buttons, inline lambdas are fine. Only matters if memoized.
issues_found.fix: Verify Inline event handlers. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.
issues_found.verification: Verify Inline event handlers. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.

broader_pattern: Inline lambdas matter only with memoization.

review_summary: Cache grows forever. No invalidation. Plus stale data won't refetch.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Caching needs TTL + LRU; use React Query.

review_summary: Rapid click = multiple buys. No idempotency.

issues_found.title: Multiple buys
issues_found.explanation: User rapid-clicks = multiple POSTs = multiple charges.

what_makes_this_review_senior: Idempotency via UI; React Query mutation.

review_summary: Cast to any defeats unknown type benefits.

issues_found.title: Defeats unknown
issues_found.explanation: After narrowing to object, still need property check.

review_summary: No aria-labelledby pointing to title. SR doesn't know title.

issues_found.title: Title not associated
issues_found.explanation: SR opens modal but doesn't read title.

review_summary: total derivable from items. State + effect = double render.

code_under_review: <input type="number" value={qty} onChange={(e) => setQty(parseInt(e.target.value))} />
review_summary: parseInt('') = NaN. Empty input = NaN state. Plus decimals lost.

issues_found.title: NaN on empty
issues_found.explanation: User clears input → NaN state → display 'NaN'.
issues_found.fix: Number() with fallback or string state

broader_pattern: Number input state typically string; parse on submit.
what_makes_this_review_senior: String state for inputs; parse + validate on submit.

review_summary: State per mousemove = 60Hz renders. Plus no cleanup.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Direct DOM for animation; bypass React render.

review_summary: 100 SVGs in JS bundle = 100KB+ in source. Should be sprite or icon component.

issues_found.title: In JS bundle
issues_found.explanation: SVG content in JS = larger bundle, no caching.

broader_pattern: SVG sprites or icon library for many icons.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Icon library or SVG sprite for many icons.

review_summary: constructor.name fragile (minification breaks). instanceof safer.
100 rows = 100 subscriptions. Better: one subscription, distribute.
Props are union of three. Type-unsafe ...spread.
apiKey in localStorage = XSS reads it. Sensitive data shouldn't be in JS-accessible storage.

issues_found.title: XSS exposure
issues_found.explanation: XSS can read localStorage. apiKey exfiltrated.

broader_pattern: Sensitive data: server-side or httpOnly only.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Sensitive data classification; httpOnly vs localStorage.

review_summary: window access in render = SSR crash.
useDeferredValue is React 18+. React 17 throws.
Rules of Hooks violation. Hooks must be called in same order.
No cleanup. Each value change starts new timer without clearing previous.

issues_found.title: Timer leak
issues_found.explanation: Value changes A→B→C: timers for A, B, C all pending. C wins but A and B fire on stale values.

review_summary: No rollback on failure. Local state diverges from server.

issues_found.title: Diverges on error
issues_found.explanation: POST fails, local state still flipped. Wrong.

what_makes_this_review_senior: Optimistic + rollback via React Query mutations.

review_summary: Returns Promise<object>. Caller has to cast every time.

issues_found.title: Useless type
issues_found.explanation: object means {} - no properties. Caller must cast.

review_summary: No nav semantics. No disabled at boundaries. No SR announcement on change.
Hook in event handler. Major rules violation.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Rules of Hooks: top level only.

review_summary: Span not button. No autoComplete. No label. No aria-pressed for toggle.
CSS scroll-snap does this natively. JS reinvention.

issues_found.title: Reinvented
issues_found.explanation: scroll-snap-type CSS does this without JS.

review_summary: MSW + handlers shipped to production. Should be dev-only.

issues_found.title: MSW in prod bundle
issues_found.explanation: MSW ~50KB + handlers ship to prod even though only for dev.

code_under_review: const isProd = window.location.hostname !== 'localhost';
review_summary: Staging detection: hostname 'staging.example.com' = neither localhost nor prod. Plus SSR fails.
